description: Hello everyone, hope your summer is going well!  I've been in DC since September of '24, and I'm not sure where to start!  Outside of work and studying for class, I'm usually at Yates Field House training for my next race, on the National Mall reffing or playing kickball (a great way to make friends outside of the program!), at The Tombs watching whatever sports team is on (Go Celtics/Rams/Bruins!) [Side Note: Tombs is the bar right next to the DSAN building and has a good happy hour from 3-6], or at my apartment hosting movie nights or playing games on Steam [Currently obsessed with Slay The Spire 2].  In terms of me personally, I'm a social guy who likes hanging out and will never say no to a good event/adventure (also a bit of a chatterbox if you couldn't tell lol).  In short, if you want an outgoing mentor who knows the city well and wants to help you get adjusted to life in DC, I'm your man!

description:
Hi, I'm so glad to be here as a mentor! A little about me: I moved to Washington, DC in 2023 to work full-time as a quantitative research assistant at a think tank, and I've loved building my life in this city ever since.

Outside of work, I'm a big believer in taking care of your whole self, not just your career. You'll often find me in a Pilates class, at a yoga studio, or joining a run club around the city. Movement is my go-to for reconnecting with my body and shaking off the stress that comes with demanding work and studies. If you're into health and wellness, we'll have plenty to talk about!

I've also navigated the real, unglamorous side of DC life: finding the right neighborhood, living with roommates, eventually living alone, and learning how this city actually works beyond the professional landscape. I know firsthand how overwhelming it can be to settle into a new city while trying to build a career, and I'd be truly honored to help make that journey smoother for you.

description: Hi and welcome to DSAN! I'm originally from Bloomfield, CT, but DC has completely stolen my heart and I'm still finding new reasons to love it every week. When I'm not buried in coursework, I'm watching films I have no business being that opinionated about, reading everything from literary fiction to books about how the world works, or hunting down the best Indian, Thai, or Jamaican food the city has to offer. I'm a pretty well-rounded person: I love being out and exploring, but I also totally get needing a quiet night in. Grad school throws a lot at you all at once, and I remember what it felt like to be new to both a program and a city! If you want someone in your corner who can help you find your footing and make this experience a lot more enjoyable, I'm your girl!

description: I'm going into my second year at DSAN and looking forward to having a few mentees to share my experience! I studied Psychology in undergrad and worked full time at a school then at a non-profit before going back to get my Master's in Data Science. It's been a challenge switching into a hard STEM field, but it's also been very rewarding and I hope to share that experience with the incoming students! I've done a lot of different research assistant experiences in the past and have a lot of experience with organizational leadership as well. I think both of these experiences have served me well in DSAN and I've enjoyed getting to continue getting more research experiences at Georgetown. I see grad school as both an academic opportunity but also a professional opportunity (i.e. helping do research with university-affiliated labs, attending conferences, building network with my peers and professors). I also grew up in the DMV area so I'm very familiar with places particularly in NOVA! I love music, arts and crafts and games and would love a mentee that wants to hang to do study dates and/or happy hours during the year!
